body: Ami — institutional custody is one of the few domains where a single infrastructure misstep doesn't create a support ticket, it creates a headline and a legal filing. At FP Block we built Kolme specifically for teams that need complete execution isolation without sacrificing interoperability — so the environment holding client assets is fully owned, not shared. Worth 20 minutes at Cannes? [link]

subject: Zodia + infrastructure ownership at Cannes
body: Ami — regulated custody is one of the only businesses where the cost of a wrong infrastructure decision isn't recoverable. Standard Chartered backing raises the stakes further: a failure at Zodia isn't just an ops problem, it's a systemic trust event. At FP Block we build dedicated execution environments for teams in exactly that position — full isolation, full ownership, no shared infrastructure risk. JB will be in Cannes and would love to connect: [link]

subject: AMI - Coffee in Cannes?
body: Hey Ami, excited to hear your talk at EthCC this year. I've been keeping up with Zodia, congrats on the support of AUDM! Let's grab coffee and connect while we're both there? If you're up for it, you can schedule with me at gofpblock.com. Cheers, JB

Sylvain — SG-Forge has done something genuinely difficult: issuing regulated security tokens on public infrastructure under full banking supervision. FP Block's Kolme framework gives issuers like SG-Forge a dedicated chain with full sovereignty over execution. Worth 20 minutes at EthCC Cannes in June?

Guillaume — noticed the Foundation dropped the 2026 Protocol Priorities the same week ETH is down 33%. The Verkle migration already demands near-perfect coordination across client teams with no clean undo path. Curious whether the decision process for sequencing those workstreams is keeping up with the roadmap's ambition. Worth a quick conversation at EthCC?
